evals.prompt: Create a new claim and prove it: the transpose of a matrix product reverses the order, i.e., (A * B)ᵀ = Bᵀ * Aᵀ
evals.expected_output: A claim.md file and a proof.lean file that compiles with zero errors and zero sorry

assertions.name: builds_clean
assertions.check: build_log.txt shows no errors and no sorry warnings

assertions.name: uses_mathlib_types
assertions.check: Uses Matrix type from Mathlib, not custom matrix definition

assertions.name: proof_structure
assertions.check: Has namespace, docstrings, and import Mathlib

evals.prompt: Prove the theorems in the provided Lean file. All three theorems have sorry placeholders that need to be replaced with valid proofs.
evals.expected_output: A proof.lean with all 3 sorry replaced by valid tactic proofs that compile cleanly

assertions.name: builds_clean
assertions.check: build_log.txt shows no errors and no sorry warnings

assertions.name: three_theorems_proved
assertions.check: All 3 theorems (mul_col_eq, harmony_per_feature_independence, harmony_correction_independent) have tactic proofs

assertions.name: proofs_chain
assertions.check: Later theorems reference earlier ones (harmony_per_feature_independence uses mul_col_eq, harmony_correction_independent uses harmony_per_feature_independence)

assertions.name: proofs_concise
assertions.check: Each proof body is at most 8 tactic lines

evals.prompt: I have a claim: For any linear map f between finite-dimensional vector spaces, the rank of f plus the nullity of f equals the dimension of the domain (rank-nullity theorem). Create the Lean skeleton for it.
evals.expected_output: A claim.md and skeleton.lean that parses cleanly (sorry warnings OK, errors not OK) using existing Mathlib types

assertions.name: has_sorry
assertions.check: skeleton.lean contains sorry (it is a skeleton, not a proof)

assertions.name: parses_clean
assertions.check: build_log.txt shows only sorry warnings, no type errors or parse errors

assertions.name: uses_existing_mathlib
assertions.check: Uses Mathlib types like LinearMap.rank, Module.finrank, LinearMap.ker, not custom definitions of rank/nullity

assertions.name: proper_type_classes
assertions.check: Includes FiniteDimensional or equivalent type class constraints
